response_parsed.opinion: It is a real and pressing issue that affects the performance of women and underrepresented minorities in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM). Research has shown that these groups experience heightened anxiety and self-doubt when they are in a position to encounter stereotype threat, leading to decreased performance and lower academic achievement. Addressing this issue is crucial for promoting diversity and inclusion in STEM fields.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Stereotype threat is a psychological phenomenon that occurs when individuals are aware of group-based expectations that conflict with their own identity. In the context of STEM, women and underrepresented minorities may experience stereotype threat due to societal expectations that these fields are dominated by men and whites. Research has shown that stereotype threat can lead to decreased performance and lower academic achievement among these groups. Addressing stereotype threat is crucial for promoting diversity and inclusion in STEM fields.
